title: Bank Reconciliation Practice
description: Given a Cash Book balance, a Bank Statement balance, and a list of transactions, identify the reconciling items and complete the Bank Reconciliation Statement.

scenarios.title: January Bank Reconciliation — GreenGrocer Ltd
scenarios.context: At 31 January, GreenGrocer Ltd's Cash Book shows a closing balance of €5,200 (Dr). The Bank Statement shows a closing balance of €5,700. You need to explain the difference.

items.description: Cheque No. 223 — paid to Murphy's Supplies Ltd, €450. Recorded in the Cash Book on 28 Jan.
items.label: Unpresented Cheque — Cheque No. 223

items.description: Cheque No. 224 — paid to O'Brien Hardware, €700. Recorded in the Cash Book on 30 Jan.
items.label: Unpresented Cheque — Cheque No. 224

items.description: Lodgement — cash receipts from customers, €800. Lodged 31 Jan, appears in Cash Book but NOT on the bank statement yet.
items.label: Outstanding Lodgement — €800

items.description: Bank charge of €75 — appears on the bank statement on 31 Jan. NOT yet entered in the Cash Book.
items.label: Bank Charge — update Cash Book

items.description: Direct credit — customer ABC Ltd paid €225 directly into your bank account on 30 Jan. NOT yet entered in the Cash Book.
items.label: Direct Credit — update Cash Book

explanations.unpresented_cheque: An unpresented (outstanding) cheque has been written and recorded in the Cash Book as a payment, but has not yet been presented to the bank for payment — so it doesn't appear on the bank statement yet. It will reduce the bank statement balance when it clears.
explanations.outstanding_lodgement: An outstanding lodgement has been recorded in the Cash Book as a receipt, but has not yet appeared on the bank statement — typically because it was lodged late in the day or on a day the bank hadn't processed it. It will increase the bank statement balance when it clears.
explanations.not_in_cashbook_deduction: This item appears on the bank statement as a deduction but has not yet been recorded in the Cash Book. You must update the Cash Book: Debit Bank Charges, Credit Bank.
explanations.not_in_cashbook_addition: This item appears on the bank statement as a credit but has not yet been recorded in the Cash Book. You must update the Cash Book: Debit Bank, Credit Debtors (or whichever account applies).

scenarios.title: March Bank Reconciliation — TechSales Ltd
scenarios.context: At 31 March, TechSales Ltd's Cash Book shows a closing Dr balance of €12,450. The Bank Statement balance is €13,200. Find the reconciling items.

items.description: Cheque No. 311 — paid to Lease Co. (equipment lease), €1,850. Recorded in Cash Book 29 Mar, not yet on statement.
items.label: Unpresented Cheque No. 311 — Lease Co.

items.description: Lodgement — customer payments lodged 31 Mar, €2,000. Appears in Cash Book but NOT on the bank statement.
items.label: Outstanding Lodgement — €2,000

items.description: Standing order — monthly insurance premium €350 debited by bank on 31 Mar. NOT entered in the Cash Book.
items.label: Standing Order (Insurance) — update Cash Book

items.description: Interest received on deposit account — €100 credited by bank 31 Mar. NOT in the Cash Book.
items.label: Interest Received — update Cash Book

explanations.unpresented_cheque: An unpresented cheque is in your Cash Book (as a payment out) but not yet shown on the bank statement. It reduces your bank statement balance when it clears.
explanations.outstanding_lodgement: An outstanding lodgement is in your Cash Book (as a receipt) but not yet on the bank statement. It will increase the bank statement balance when the bank processes it.
explanations.not_in_cashbook_deduction: A standing order or direct debit that the bank has processed but you haven't recorded yet. Update the Cash Book: Debit the expense account, Credit Bank.
explanations.not_in_cashbook_addition: Bank interest or a direct credit that appears on the statement but not in your Cash Book. Update the Cash Book: Debit Bank, Credit Interest Received.
